metadata.summary: Organized bash alias methodology: simple aliases vs functions, safety flags, modern-tool gating, tab completion, and 60+ categorized shortcuts.

content.title: Bash Aliases for Solo Developer Productivity
content.summary: Organized bash alias and function methodology for solo developer servers: categorized ~/.bash_aliases with sections for system, navigation, git, Docker, systemd, nginx, network, logs, and project-specific shortcuts. Distinguishes simple aliases from function aliases, covers completion-aware wrappers, and provides a complete 60+ alias reference.

why.p: A well-organized alias file reduces repetitive typing by 30+ keystrokes per common operation, prevents mistakes via safety aliases for destructive commands, and documents common operations in a human-readable runbook format. The aliases are the difference between `systemctl --user status nero-core nero-channel-web` and `nero-status`.

list.item: Setting up a new server or workstation where productivity shortcuts are missing
Onboarding to a new project where project-specific aliases speed up repetitive tasks
Auditing existing .bash_aliases for conflicts, missing completions, or undocumented shortcuts
Consolidating ad-hoc functions scattered across .bashrc into organized alias files
Environments where shell config is managed by Ansible/Chef — use those tools instead
Shared servers with multiple users where one developer's aliases conflict with others
Scripts and pipelines needing portability across shells (zsh, fish, sh)
Replacing proper scripts: complex multi-step operations belong in ~/bin/ scripts

rule.statement: Use simple aliases for flag additions and substitutions; use shell functions when you need parameters, conditionals, or multi-step operations.
rule.rationale: Aliases cannot accept positional parameters. Attempting to do so leads to brittle workarounds. Functions handle parameters natively and are easier to debug.

rule.statement: Always add safety confirmation flags to destructive commands: rm -i, mv -i, cp -i.
rule.rationale: A single absent confirmation on rm -rf on a production server has no undo. The interactive flag adds one extra keystroke in exchange for a meaningful safety net.

rule.statement: Gate modern-tool aliases behind command existence checks so the file loads without errors on systems where those tools are not installed.
rule.rationale: The same ~/.bash_aliases is often shared across machines via dotfiles. A missing bat or eza causes a hard error on every new shell if not gated with command -v.

example.title: Simple aliases vs function aliases
Alias anti-patterns
example.code:
# Wrong: complex logic in alias — use a function instead alias mything="cmd1; cmd2; if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then cmd3; fi"
# Wrong: no existence check, fails on minimal systems alias cat="bat --paging=never" alias ls="eza --color=always"
example.note: Aliases are not functions. Any branching, parameters, or multi-step logic belongs in a shell function.

list.item: Single-letter abbreviations (g=git, dk=docker) — only for the most frequently used commands
Prefix+action pattern (dk-ps, sc-restart) — for grouped command families
Descriptive verb-noun (nero-status, show-ports) — for infrequently used but memorable shortcuts
Avoid shadowing common system commands unless the alias adds universal improvements (grep, diff)

example.title: Gate modern tools with existence checks
example.code:
# Gate eza aliases behind existence check if command -v eza &>/dev/null; then alias ls="eza --color=always --group-directories-first" alias ll="eza -la --color=always --group-directories-first --git" alias lt="eza --tree --level=2 --color=always" else alias ll="ls -alF --color=auto" alias la="ls -A --color=auto" fi
# Gate bat alias if command -v bat &>/dev/null; then alias cat="bat --paging=never" fi

rule.statement: Wire tab completion for short aliases using complete -F so git, docker, and systemctl completions work with single-letter aliases.
rule.rationale: Without completion wiring, tab completion does not complete to git status. Re-wiring completions is a one-time setup that multiplies the value of short aliases.

example.title: Preserve tab completion for aliases
example.code:
# Preserve git completion for g alias alias g="git" if declare -F __git_complete &>/dev/null; then __git_complete g git fi
# After defining: source ~/.bash_aliases to activate

antipattern.description: Aliasing a command to itself with different flags, creating an infinite recursion loop.
antipattern.reason: alias rm="rm -rf" calls rm which calls rm again infinitely until the process limit is hit, making rm unusable. Use a different name or a function with \rm to bypass the alias.

antipattern.description: Missing quotes around alias values containing spaces or special characters.
antipattern.reason: alias ll=ls -la (unquoted) only aliases ll to ls; the -la is parsed as a separate token and ignored. The alias silently misbehaves rather than throwing an error.

antipattern.description: Putting complex logic (if/else, loops, subshells) directly in alias values.
antipattern.reason: Bash aliases do not support compound commands. They appear to work in simple cases but break in subtle ways with quoting, piping, and argument expansion. Use functions for any logic.

antipattern.description: Forgetting to source ~/.bash_aliases after making changes and wondering why new aliases do not work.
antipattern.reason: Aliases are loaded at shell startup. Editing the file does not update the running shell. Use `source ~/.bash_aliases` or a reload alias defined early in the file.
Mixing aliases into .bashrc makes the file harder to manage, harder to sync via dotfiles, and prevents isolated reload with a single source command.

example.title: Safe alias patterns
Unsafe alias patterns
example.code:
# Wrong: circular alias — infinite recursion alias rm="rm -rf"
# Wrong: unquoted, the flags are dropped silently alias ll=ls -la
# Wrong: complex logic in alias, breaks with pipes alias deploy="cd /srv/app && git pull && make install && systemctl restart app" # Correct version: define as a function deploy() { cd /srv/app && git pull && make install && systemctl restart app; }
# Wrong: aliases scattered in .bashrc between unrelated config export PATH="$HOME/bin:$PATH" alias gs="git status" # Don't put this here export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"

template.purpose: Complete categorized ~/.bash_aliases with 60+ aliases and functions
